iterations.hypothesis: This strategy combines RSI for momentum confirmation with Bollinger Bands for volatility-based entry signals, using ATR for dynamic risk management. The RSI identifies overbought/oversold conditions, while Bollinger Bands provide clear entry points when price touches the lower/upper bands. ATR-based stops and take-profits ensure position sizing adapts to market volatility, reducing risk during high-volatility periods and maximizing gains during stable conditions.

The previous strategy failed because it generated excessive false signals during ranging markets and lacked trend confirmation. This improved version adds a trend filter using ADX to avoid trading against the trend, and refines entry rules to only trigger when price is near bollinger bands with confirmed momentum. The combination reduces whipsaw losses while maintaining profitable setups.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy combines RSI for momentum confirmation with Bollinger Bands for volatility-based entry signals, while ATR ensures dynamic risk management. The RSI identifies overbought/oversold conditions, Bollinger Bands provide price reversion opportunities when price touches lower/upper bands, and ATR-based stop-loss/take-profit ensures position sizing and risk control. This combination reduces false signals while maintaining responsiveness to market volatility.

Adding a trend filter using ADX will reduce false signals during ranging markets, improving signal quality and reducing whipsaw losses. The combination of RSI for momentum and Bollinger Bands for volatility-based entry, filtered by ADX for trend strength, creates a more robust system that avoids trading against the trend. ATR-based stop-loss and take-profit ensure proper risk management with dynamic sizing.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy leverages RSI for momentum confirmation and Bollinger Bands for volatility-adjusted entry signals, while ATR provides dynamic stop-loss and take-profit levels. The combination exploits mean-reversion opportunities when price touches Bollinger Bands, with RSI confirming whether the move is overbought or oversold. ATR-based risk management ensures position sizing adapts to market volatility, reducing drawdown during high-volatility periods.

Adding a trend filter using ADX prevents entering trades against the prevailing trend, which was causing high drawdowns in previous iterations. The combination of RSI for momentum, Bollinger Bands for mean reversion signals, and ATR for dynamic risk management creates a robust framework. The ADX filter ensures that entries only occur when trend strength exceeds a threshold, reducing whipsaw losses and improving win rate while maintaining profitability.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy leverages RSI for momentum confirmation and Bollinger Bands for volatility-based entry signals, while ATR provides dynamic risk management. The combination exploits mean-reversion opportunities when price touches Bollinger Band extremes, with RSI confirming oversold/overbought conditions. ATR-based stops and take-profits ensure proper risk-reward ratios and prevent large drawdowns during volatile market conditions.

Combining RSI with Bollinger Bands creates a robust mean-reversion signal, but adding ATR-based filters improves entry quality by filtering out low-volatility environments where price movements are less reliable. The ATR also enables dynamic stop-loss and take-profit levels that adapt to market volatility, reducing whipsaw losses and improving risk-reward ratios.

Combining Supertrend with RSI creates a robust filter that reduces false signals while maintaining trend-following efficiency. Supertrend provides dynamic support/resistance levels and trend direction, while RSI confirms momentum at entry points. This reduces whipsaw losses from pure Bollinger-based strategies and prevents overtrading during ranging markets. The ATR-based risk management ensures position sizing and stop-loss/take-profit levels scale with volatility, preventing large drawdowns during market turbulence.

This strategy combines RSI for momentum confirmation with Bollinger Bands for volatility-based entry signals, while ATR ensures robust risk management. The RSI identifies overbought/oversold conditions, Bollinger Bands provide dynamic support/resistance levels, and ATR-based stops protect against adverse price movements. The combination creates a disciplined approach that avoids false breakouts while maintaining sensitivity to trend changes.
